name: spice_socialwave
_what: Dynamic 'spice' pro captions — reverse-engineered from the reference editor (the team's best editor). The locked pro_locked/speaker_canon path is single-axis (Regular->Extrabold weight + optional yellow numbers). THIS path is multi-axis: a full Montserrat weight palette used as an emphasis dial, gentle (un-leaned) italics, and per-word voice color. Driven by generate_spice.py + a per-word style stream (hand- or LLM-authored 'caption director'). See references/spice_caption_spec.md.
_fonts_note: Full Adobe-licensed Montserrat family, extracted from the user's CoreSync cache and renamed so each weight is its own libass family. fonts_dir is relative to the skill root. These are the user's licensed fonts — do NOT web-download Montserrat.
_weights_note: LOCKED 2026-06-10 (Operator): default caption weight is Montserrat MEDIUM — NEVER Regular/Light/Thin. A thin Regular base made the shadow read wrong (not enough glyph mass). Emphasis ladder is Medium(base) -> Bold -> Extrabold -> Black, plus italics. mute/base/soft all floor at Medium so the text never goes lighter than Medium.
_fontsize_note: LOCKED 2026-06-10 (Operator): 150px at 4K (2160 wide). (250 was WAY too big — Operator; the one the reference editor frame that looked larger was an outlier, don't size off a single frame.)
_size_note: SIZE axis measured from the reference editor's 19 clips (1271 captions): ~27% bumped above base; among bumps p50~1.28 / p75~1.36 / p90~1.60 / peak~1.8-2.0. base=100; emph=125 (light, the common bump); strong=150; peak=185 (rare payoff word/line). Triggers: numbers/money, the payoff/punchline, key/identity & proper nouns, vivid words. Rendered via \fscx/\fscy on the word — INDEPENDENT of color/weight/italic, they STACK (e.g. $100M = yellow + extrabold + peak). HARD RULE (user 2026-06-03): a size bump may ONLY land on a SINGLE-WORD caption — generate_spice gates size on len(chunk)==1; multi-word lines emphasize via WEIGHT only (mixed sizes read broken / can wrap to two lines). To bump a specific word, the director isolates it into its own caption.
_size_correction_2026-06-11: SIZE AXIS ON, but SUBTLE (Operator 2026-06-11): a size bump is a 10-15% increase, NOT 25-80%. The big tiers (125/150/180) pushed lines off-screen — 'be at $10M minimum' at 150% = 99% of frame width, out of the UI safe zone. New tiers: emph 110 / strong 115 / peak 120 (the rare single-word punch). RULES: (1) size is decided PER CUE — single-word caption OR the whole line uniformly, NEVER one word inside a multi-word line (looks broken; generate_spice enforces). (2) A SAFE-ZONE CAP in generate_spice measures each bumped line (PIL) and reduces the bump so the rendered width never exceeds ~82% of frame — captions can't run off-screen or out of the UI regardless of word count. Size stacks with weight + italic.
_italic_note: Gentle x-shear (\fax) instead of synthetic oblique (\i1). -0.12 ~= 7deg forward/RIGHT (positive fax leans LEFT/backslant in libass); \i1 ~= 12deg read too leaned (user 2026-06-03). True italic .otf files are also in fonts_dir if real italic letterforms are ever wanted.
_color_note: COLOR = whose voice. White = Speaker's own voice. Yellow = a different voice (live Q&A guest, or Speaker impersonating a customer/objection). Drive via the style stream's per-word 'c' or top-level voice_spans.
_layout_note: y 50% = mid-frame, DATA-DRIVEN (2026-06-10): measured across the reference editor's 24 workshop Q&A clips (599 shots, training/height_study) — his overall median caption-Y is ~49.7%, his dominant medium/closeup tight-shot anchor is ~50%, and he NEVER goes below ~50%. (Old 55 sat too low.) This static default is for renders with NO layout track; when a layout track IS present (caption-app, and /edit which now runs layout_analyze on its reframed output), each shot's Y is per-angle: WIDE 45% / TIGHT 50%. Chunk caps LOCKED: <=18 displayed chars AND <=3 words, hard-broken at every sentence (.?!) and clause (,). For 1080x1920 set video 1080x1920 and font_size_px ~75.
_shadow_note: Premiere Drop Shadow — LOCKED 2026-06-10, built from the reference editor's actual preset SLIDERS (Operator screenshot) + picked by Operator on his own clip. TWO layers, each = Opacity/Angle/Distance/Size/Blur. The decode lesson: the preset's two shadows are (1) Opacity100 Angle130 Distance20 Size15 BLUR110, (2) Opacity70 Angle135 Distance7 Size33.9 BLUR250 — the BLUR is HUGE (110/250), which is why a tight low-blur shadow looked like a 'stroke'. Mapped to gblur @FS150: ambient layer (premiere_*) = big soft halo σ75/border20(Size)/0.70 op, small offset (6,8); directional layer (premiere_*2) = σ33/border9(Size)/1.0 op with a real DOWN-RIGHT offset (18,22) — Operator: 'more X and Y offset to the right and down'. border here encodes Premiere 'Size'(spread); offset encodes Distance@Angle (down-right). All FONT-RELATIVE (FS_REF=150). premiere_blur->gblur sigma ≈ blur×0.3 (the conversion that matched).

shadow._locked: SHADOW LOCKED 2026-06-11 (Operator: 'we finally figured out a shadow, lock it in everywhere'): two-layer gblur Premiere drop shadow, TIGHTENED softness/size — sigma 48/20, border(spread) 13/6 (was 75/33, 20/9 — the dark halo hugs the letters, more defined, less diffuse), opacity UNCHANGED (intensity 0.70/1.0), down-right offset. Values are FONT-RELATIVE (scaled by FS/150 in generate_spice) so 4K and 1080 render identically. This is THE default shadow for every footage type. Approved on _guest_1080_shadowtight.mp4.

animation._note: Speaker Shorts 'Text Down Small': drop ~16px from above over 500ms + fade 90%->100% over 83ms. (16px at 4K ~= 8px at 1080.)

timing._note: onset_correct = hold a caption through a pause Groq folded into the next FUNCTION word (fixes the 'irrelevant flashes then because shows during the silence' bug). min_cue_dur = no caption may flash shorter than this; zero-gap = each cue ends exactly when the next begins.

auto_emphasis._note: Light DEFAULT styling when no per-word style stream is supplied, so solo clips are reasonable without the director. The director (style JSON) overrides this entirely.

_brand: Social Wave brand preset. Base = WHITE (speaker voice). EMPHASIS/PAYOFF punch words are colored in the two Social Wave brand accents, ALTERNATING blue<->coral, uppercased, size-bumped. This repurposes the color axis from voice->emphasis for single-speaker brand clips. The director brand note (references/socialwave_caption_note.md) tells the LLM director to paint payoff words in 'brand_blue'/'brand_coral' alternately instead of reserving color for guest voice.
_emphasis_color_policy: SOCIAL WAVE: the single biggest PAYOFF word on a line (or a 1-word cue) is colored brand_blue or brand_coral, ALTERNATING line-to-line, ALL-CAPS, + a size bump. Base stays white. NO emojis in captions ever. If a real 2nd speaker exists (Q&A), fall back to guest=yellow for that voice.
